regulatory_references.name: ISO 20000
regulatory_references.relevance: International standard for service management systems

compliance_patterns.description: Service design engagements must define measurable SLAs with monitoring mechanisms

evaluator_insights.insight: Service design RFPs value user-centric approaches with evidence of user research
evaluator_insights.implication: Methodology must include user research and journey mapping phases
evaluator_insights.recommended_action: Include user research methods: interviews, journey mapping, service safaris, prototype testing

evaluator_insights.insight: Evaluators look for measurable service improvement targets
evaluator_insights.implication: Proposals must quantify expected improvements in service quality, efficiency, or satisfaction
evaluator_insights.recommended_action: Include baseline assessment and target KPIs for service quality metrics

evaluator_insights.insight: Service portfolio rationalization is often an implicit requirement
evaluator_insights.implication: Even when not stated, evaluators expect assessment of current service portfolio efficiency
evaluator_insights.recommended_action: Include service portfolio assessment in the diagnostic phase

methodology_patterns.when_to_use: User-centric service portfolio development and redesign

forbidden_assumptions: Do NOT assume the client has an existing service catalog — discovery must come first
Do NOT design services without user research — internal assumptions about user needs are unreliable
Do NOT conflate service design with IT service management — service design covers all organizational services
